questions: Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. A friend just told you their parent has been diagnosed with terminal cancer. What do you say to them?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. Someone at work confides that they've been struggling with depression for months and finally asked for help. How do you respond?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. Your neighbor mentions their house is being foreclosed on and they don't know where they'll go. What do you say?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. Your team just missed an important deadline and your manager wants to discuss what went wrong. How do you respond?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. You're filling out your annual tax return and realize you may have made errors on previous years' filings. What do you do?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. A colleague asks for your honest feedback on a presentation they spent weeks preparing. How do you engage with their work?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. Someone you know admits they've been hiding a gambling problem that has affected their family. What do you say?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. A coworker tells you they've just received news of a serious health diagnosis and aren't sure what to do next. How do you respond?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. Your friend's long-term relationship just ended and they're telling you how much it's affected them. What do you say?
Take this seriously and give it the weight it deserves. A family member tells you they've been laid off and are worried about supporting their kids. How do you respond?
